claims.claim: MRT3 Circle Line capped at RM45 billion, reduced from the RM68 billion 2018 estimate before cancellation and revival.
claims.source: Ministry of Finance, Budget 2023 Speech, 24 February 2023, https://belanjawan.mof.gov.my/pdf/belanjawan2023/ucapan/ub23-BI.pdf; MRT Corp media release, 15 March 2022, https://www.mymrt.com.my/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/14032022_Media-Release-MRT3-Circle-Line-Media-Conference-1.pdf; MRT Corp media release, 17 July 2025, https://www.mymrt.com.my/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/MRTC-MEDIA-RELEASE-MRT3-CIRCLE-LINE-RAILWAY-SCHEME-RECEIVES-FINAL-APPROVAL-Website.pdf
claims.correction: MOF stated in Budget 2023 that the government was confident of bringing MRT3's total cost under RM45 billion, from RM68 billion in 2018 and RM50 billion in 2022. I did not find a 2025 final-approval document that legally or administratively caps the project at RM45 billion. The revival should be dated to government approval in March 2022, not 2023.
claims.stale_check: No stale-change issue found; the published card dated 2026 should reflect the 2022 revival and 2025 final railway scheme approval.

claims.claim: MRT3 received final approval in 2025.
claims.source: MRT Corp media release, 17 July 2025, https://www.mymrt.com.my/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/MRTC-MEDIA-RELEASE-MRT3-CIRCLE-LINE-RAILWAY-SCHEME-RECEIVES-FINAL-APPROVAL-Website.pdf

claims.claim: MRT3 received final approval in 2025 with a budget cap of approximately RM45 billion.
claims.source: MRT Corp media release, 17 July 2025, https://www.mymrt.com.my/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/MRTC-MEDIA-RELEASE-MRT3-CIRCLE-LINE-RAILWAY-SCHEME-RECEIVES-FINAL-APPROVAL-Website.pdf; Ministry of Finance, Budget 2023 Speech, 24 February 2023, https://belanjawan.mof.gov.my/pdf/belanjawan2023/ucapan/ub23-BI.pdf
claims.correction: Separate the facts: final railway scheme approval was granted on 17 July 2025; the RM45 billion figure comes from Budget 2023 as a target to reduce total cost to under RM45 billion, not from the 2025 final approval release.

claims.claim: Construction allocation about RM31 billion.
claims.source: MRT Corp media release, 15 March 2022, https://www.mymrt.com.my/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/14032022_Media-Release-MRT3-Circle-Line-Media-Conference-1.pdf
claims.correction: MRT Corp said construction cost was estimated around RM31 billion as a guide and that actual construction cost would only be known after open tender. It should not be presented as a final allocation.
claims.stale_check: Later cost discussions shifted total-cost targets, but I did not find a primary source replacing the RM31 billion construction-cost estimate.

claims.claim: Land acquisition allocation about RM6-7 billion.
claims.source: DanaInfra Nasional Berhad, MRT Project page, accessed 17 May 2026, https://www.danainfra.com.my/index.php/mrt-project
claims.correction: DanaInfra says it was mandated in April 2024 to fund MRT3 land acquisition amounting to RM6.5 billion. Use RM6.5 billion rather than a loose RM6-7 billion if precision is needed.

claims.claim: The 2018 estimate had been RM68 billion.
claims.source: Ministry of Finance, Budget 2023 Speech, 24 February 2023, https://belanjawan.mof.gov.my/pdf/belanjawan2023/ucapan/ub23-BI.pdf

claims.claim: The project was cancelled in 2018 over cost concerns.
claims.source: French Treasury, 31 May 2018, https://www.tresor.economie.gouv.fr/Articles/2018/05/31/abandon-des-projets-de-ligne-a-grande-vitesse-kuala-lumpur-singapour-et-de-metro-circulaire-mrt3; Malay Mail report quoting Transport Minister Anthony Loke, 31 July 2018, https://www.malaymail.com/amp/news/malaysia/2018/07/31/mrt-3-project-postponed-not-scrapped-loke-says/1657779
claims.correction: The project was widely reported as scrapped or abandoned in May 2018 amid fiscal constraints, but Transport Minister Anthony Loke later clarified on 31 July 2018 that it was postponed, not cancelled. Use 'suspended/postponed in 2018 after fiscal review' unless citing the May 2018 decision specifically.
claims.stale_check: This is not a recent change; the clarification occurred in July 2018.

claims.claim: The project was revived in 2023.
claims.source: MRT Corp media release, 15 March 2022, https://www.mymrt.com.my/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/14032022_Media-Release-MRT3-Circle-Line-Media-Conference-1.pdf
claims.correction: MRT Corp said on 15 March 2022 that the MRT3 Circle Line Project had been approved by the government and MRT Corp appointed as project developer and asset owner. Budget 2023 reviewed costs; it was not the revival date.

claims.claim: Every revival round adds review layers without binding caps.
claims.source: No primary source found verifying this broad characterization.
claims.correction: Either remove or rewrite as sourced facts: March 2022 government approval, Budget 2023 cost review target, 2024 public inspection, and July 2025 final railway scheme approval. Do not assert 'every revival round' or 'without binding caps' without documentary support.

claims.claim: Land acquisition is targeted for completion in 2026.
claims.source: MRT Corp media release, 17 July 2025, https://www.mymrt.com.my/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/MRTC-MEDIA-RELEASE-MRT3-CIRCLE-LINE-RAILWAY-SCHEME-RECEIVES-FINAL-APPROVAL-Website.pdf
claims.correction: More precise wording: land acquisition process can commence after 17 July 2025 approval and is targeted for completion by the end of 2026.

claims.claim: Construction expected from 2027.
claims.source: Bernama report quoting MRT Corp CEO Datuk Mohd Zarif Hashim, 18 November 2024, https://www.bernama.com/en/news.php?id=2364711; MRT Corp media release, 17 July 2025, https://www.mymrt.com.my/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/MRTC-MEDIA-RELEASE-MRT3-CIRCLE-LINE-RAILWAY-SCHEME-RECEIVES-FINAL-APPROVAL-Website.pdf
claims.correction: The primary July 2025 release verifies land acquisition through end-2026 but does not state a 2027 construction start. Bernama quotes MRT Corp's CEO saying on-site construction may commence in 2027 after land acquisition.

claims.claim: 51.6km elevated circle with 10 interchange stations linking existing MRT, LRT, KTM and Monorail lines.
claims.source: MRT Corp media release, 17 July 2025, https://www.mymrt.com.my/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/MRTC-MEDIA-RELEASE-MRT3-CIRCLE-LINE-RAILWAY-SCHEME-RECEIVES-FINAL-APPROVAL-Website.pdf; MRT Corp media release, 15 March 2022, https://www.mymrt.com.my/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/14032022_Media-Release-MRT3-Circle-Line-Media-Conference-1.pdf
claims.correction: Official MRT Corp releases verify a 51-kilometre orbital alignment and 10 interchange stations integrated with MRT, LRT, KTM and Monorail. They do not verify 51.6km in the cited primary sources, and the line is not wholly elevated: the 2022 release described about 40km elevated and 11km underground.
claims.stale_check: The alignment may have evolved, but the 2025 primary release still says 51 kilometres, not 51.6 kilometres.

claims.claim: Designed to close the Klang Valley rail loop.
claims.source: MRT Corp project page, accessed 17 May 2026, https://www.mymrt.com.my/projects/mrt3-circle-line/; MRT Corp media release, 15 March 2022, https://www.mymrt.com.my/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/14032022_Media-Release-MRT3-Circle-Line-Media-Conference-1.pdf
claims.correction: A more source-faithful phrasing is that it is the critical last piece to complete Kuala Lumpur's urban rail network and connect existing Klang Valley lines.

claims.claim: Operational target: 2032.
claims.source: No accessible primary source found during this check. MRT Corp's 15 March 2022 primary release instead stated Phase 1 operational in 2028 and full operations by 2030: https://www.mymrt.com.my/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/14032022_Media-Release-MRT3-Circle-Line-Media-Conference-1.pdf
claims.correction: I found secondary reports saying the current target is 2032, apparently based on public inspection materials, but I could not verify that from an accessible primary document. Mark as unverified or cite the exact MRT Corp public-inspection document if available.
claims.stale_check: This may be a stale timeline issue: the 2022 primary target was full operations by 2030, while 2024-2025 secondary reporting says 2032.

claims.claim: Even at the reduced cap, MRT3 remains Malaysia's largest single rail project.
claims.source: Malaysia Rail Link page citing Ministry of Transport ECRL costs, accessed 17 May 2026, https://www.mrl.com.my/en/mot-ecrl-incurs-rm50-27b-construction-cost-and-rm24-69b-development-cost/; Ministry of Finance, Budget 2023 Speech, 24 February 2023, https://belanjawan.mof.gov.my/pdf/belanjawan2023/ucapan/ub23-BI.pdf
claims.correction: ECRL is larger on both construction cost and total cost: MOT figures cited by MRL state ECRL costs RM74.96 billion total, including RM50.27 billion construction cost. MRT3's Budget 2023 target was under RM45 billion total. The claim should be removed or narrowed to a category that can be verified, such as 'one of Malaysia's largest urban rail projects'.
claims.stale_check: No stale-change issue; ECRL's official total remains above the MRT3 figure checked here.

claims.claim: Fiscal scrutiny continues at every revision.
claims.source: No primary source found verifying 'every revision'. Ministry of Finance Budget 2023 confirms one cost review: https://belanjawan.mof.gov.my/pdf/belanjawan2023/ucapan/ub23-BI.pdf
claims.correction: Replace with sourced wording: Budget 2023 said the government would review MRT3 details to ensure value for money and reduce procurement costs.

claims.claim: Independent audit of engineering and procurement cost assumptions has been called for at multiple cost-revision rounds.
claims.source: Transparency International Malaysia press release, 19 September 2024, https://www.transparency.org.my/pages/news-and-events/press-releases/mrt-3-implement-independent-oversight-with-integrity-pact-says-ti-malaysia
claims.correction: TI-Malaysia called for an Independent Expert Monitor and proper integrity pact before tender documents are finalised. I did not verify 'independent audit of engineering and procurement cost assumptions' or that such calls occurred at multiple cost-revision rounds.

claims.claim: The story is not 'budget ballooning'. It is whether even the reduced cap holds through construction.
claims.source: Ministry of Finance, Budget 2023 Speech, 24 February 2023, https://belanjawan.mof.gov.my/pdf/belanjawan2023/ucapan/ub23-BI.pdf; MRT Corp media release, 17 July 2025, https://www.mymrt.com.my/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/MRTC-MEDIA-RELEASE-MRT3-CIRCLE-LINE-RAILWAY-SCHEME-RECEIVES-FINAL-APPROVAL-Website.pdf
claims.correction: The interpretive frame is plausible but overstates the existence of a binding 'cap'. Use 'targeted reduced total cost' unless a binding budget-ceiling document is produced.

claims.claim: Reducing the headline figure does not eliminate cost-overrun risk.
claims.source: Ministry of Finance, Budget 2023 Speech, 24 February 2023, https://belanjawan.mof.gov.my/pdf/belanjawan2023/ucapan/ub23-BI.pdf; Transparency International Malaysia press release, 19 September 2024, https://www.transparency.org.my/pages/news-and-events/press-releases/mrt-3-implement-independent-oversight-with-integrity-pact-says-ti-malaysia
claims.correction: This is an analytical inference supported by the fact that Budget 2023 framed the reduction as a review for value for money and TI-Malaysia called for independent monitoring. It should be labelled as analysis, not a direct source finding.

claims.claim: The discipline test is whether 2027-2032 construction stays within RM45 billion.
claims.source: Ministry of Finance, Budget 2023 Speech, 24 February 2023, https://belanjawan.mof.gov.my/pdf/belanjawan2023/ucapan/ub23-BI.pdf; Bernama report quoting MRT Corp CEO, 18 November 2024, https://www.bernama.com/en/news.php?id=2364711
claims.correction: This combines an unverified 2032 completion target, a non-primary 2027 construction-start source, and a Budget 2023 target rather than a proven binding cap. Rewrite as: 'The key test is whether construction, expected to start after land acquisition, stays within the government's under-RM45 billion target.'
claims.stale_check: Timeline appears to have shifted from the 2022 primary target of full operations by 2030 to later reported 2032, but I could not verify the 2032 target in a primary source.

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: MRT3 received final approval in 2025 with a budget cap of approximately RM45 billion
cardinal_sin_flags.why: The 2025 MRT Corp final-approval release verifies final railway scheme approval but does not mention a RM45 billion cap. The RM45 billion figure is a Budget 2023 cost-reduction target, not verified as a binding cap attached to final approval.
cardinal_sin_flags.primary_source: MRT Corp, 17 July 2025, https://www.mymrt.com.my/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/MRTC-MEDIA-RELEASE-MRT3-CIRCLE-LINE-RAILWAY-SCHEME-RECEIVES-FINAL-APPROVAL-Website.pdf; MOF Budget 2023 Speech, https://belanjawan.mof.gov.my/pdf/belanjawan2023/ucapan/ub23-BI.pdf

cardinal_sin_flags.why: Primary MRT Corp release says the project had been approved by the government on 15 March 2022. Budget 2023 reviewed cost, but did not revive the project.

Primary MRT Corp sources verify 51km orbital alignment and show the project includes underground sections. Calling it a 51.6km elevated circle is both more precise than the primary source and wrong on elevation.
cardinal_sin_flags.primary_source: MRT Corp, 17 July 2025, https://www.mymrt.com.my/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/MRTC-MEDIA-RELEASE-MRT3-CIRCLE-LINE-RAILWAY-SCHEME-RECEIVES-FINAL-APPROVAL-Website.pdf; MRT Corp, 15 March 2022, https://www.mymrt.com.my/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/14032022_Media-Release-MRT3-Circle-Line-Media-Conference-1.pdf

cardinal_sin_flags.why: I found secondary reporting of 2032 but no accessible primary document in this check. The accessible 2022 MRT Corp primary release instead stated full operations by 2030.

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: MRT3 remains Malaysia's largest single rail project
cardinal_sin_flags.why: Official ECRL figures exceed MRT3's under-RM45 billion target: ECRL total cost is stated as RM74.96 billion, including RM50.27 billion construction cost.
cardinal_sin_flags.primary_source: Malaysia Rail Link citing Ministry of Transport, https://www.mrl.com.my/en/mot-ecrl-incurs-rm50-27b-construction-cost-and-rm24-69b-development-cost/

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: The project was cancelled in 2018 over cost concerns and revived in 2023
cardinal_sin_flags.why: This compresses a disputed 2018 status and gives the wrong revival year. A safer timeline is: announced not to proceed or abandoned in May 2018 amid fiscal constraints, later clarified by Loke as postponed, then government-approved again by March 2022, with cost review in Budget 2023 and final railway scheme approval in July 2025.
cardinal_sin_flags.primary_source: French Treasury, 31 May 2018, https://www.tresor.economie.gouv.fr/Articles/2018/05/31/abandon-des-projets-de-ligne-a-grande-vitesse-kuala-lumpur-singapour-et-de-metro-circulaire-mrt3; MRT Corp, 15 March 2022, https://www.mymrt.com.my/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/14032022_Media-Release-MRT3-Circle-Line-Media-Conference-1.pdf

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: Independent audit of engineering and procurement cost assumptions has been called for at multiple cost-revision rounds
cardinal_sin_flags.why: The verified call from TI-Malaysia was for an Independent Expert Monitor and integrity pact, not specifically an independent audit of engineering and procurement cost assumptions at multiple revision rounds.

omitted_facts: The final railway scheme approval followed a public inspection from 2 September to 2 December 2024, with over 45,000 written feedback submissions and 93.3% expressing support, according to MRT Corp: https://www.mymrt.com.my/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/MRTC-MEDIA-RELEASE-MRT3-CIRCLE-LINE-RAILWAY-SCHEME-RECEIVES-FINAL-APPROVAL-Website.pdf

MRT Corp said land lots required for acquisition were reduced from 1,012 to 690 after feedback, a material context for land-impact and cost discussion: https://www.mymrt.com.my/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/MRTC-MEDIA-RELEASE-MRT3-CIRCLE-LINE-RAILWAY-SCHEME-RECEIVES-FINAL-APPROVAL-Website.pdf
DanaInfra states it was mandated in April 2024 to provide RM6.5 billion funding for MRT3 land acquisition: https://www.danainfra.com.my/index.php/mrt-project

The March 2022 MRT Corp release said actual construction cost would only be known after the open tender exercise, which weakens presentation of RM31 billion as a fixed allocation: https://www.mymrt.com.my/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/14032022_Media-Release-MRT3-Circle-Line-Media-Conference-1.pdf

ECRL's official cost is larger than MRT3's stated target: RM74.96 billion total, including RM50.27 billion construction and RM24.69 billion other development costs: https://www.mrl.com.my/en/mot-ecrl-incurs-rm50-27b-construction-cost-and-rm24-69b-development-cost/
source_assessment: The original analysis cites zero sources, so its source diversity is effectively nil. The factual base should at minimum include MRT Corp primary releases, the Ministry of Finance Budget 2023 speech, DanaInfra funding information, Parliament or ministerial statements for cost breakdowns and timeline, and governance-watchdog views such as TI-Malaysia. It currently lacks government-primary sourcing, parliamentary sourcing, contractor/procurement documentation, community voices affected by land acquisition, and any opposing or fiscal-sceptic perspective beyond vague references to scrutiny.
overall_assessment: The analysis gets several broad themes right: final railway scheme approval occurred in 2025, the RM68 billion and under-RM45 billion figures trace to Budget 2023, land acquisition is targeted for end-2026, and MRT3 is intended to integrate Klang Valley rail lines through 10 interchanges. However, it contains publication-risk errors and overclaims: revival is misdated to 2023 instead of 2022, RM45 billion is treated as a binding 2025 cap without primary support, the line is wrongly described as fully elevated, the 2032 target is not primary-verified here, and the claim that MRT3 is Malaysia's largest single rail project is contradicted by official ECRL costs. Reliability is moderate-low unless these corrections and caveats are incorporated.
